channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Osun poll result will reflect people’s will, says INEC
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
INEC assures stakeholders the August 15 Osun election results will reflect the will of the people, promising a credible, free, and inclusive poll.
Read More: https://punchng.com/osun-poll-result-will-reflect-peoples-will-says-inec/

item.title: Nigeria’s net FX reserves rise from $3bn to $40bn — Cardoso
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s net FX reserves have surged from $3bn to $40bn, reflecting improved external liquidity and investor confidence, according to CBN Governor Cardoso
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerias-net-fx-reserves-rise-from-3bn-to-40bn-cardoso/

item.title: Bayelsa Utd unveils Maikaba as new coach, targets NPFL return
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bayelsa United appoints Abdul Maikaba as new coach, tasked with immediate NPFL promotion. The government emphasizes local talent development for the club’s
Read More: https://punchng.com/bayelsa-utd-unveils-maikaba-as-new-coach-targets-npfl-return/

item.title: Delta to receive 50 CNG buses in five months — Commissioner
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Delta State is set to receive 50 CNG buses within five months, launching a new mass transit system. These buses will offer cheaper, safer, and more comfort
Read More: https://punchng.com/delta-to-receive-50-cng-buses-in-five-months-commissioner/

item.title: PDP faults Diri’s N100bn stadium project, demands jobs, food security
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bayelsa PDP criticises Governor Diri’s N100bn stadium project, demanding focus on job creation, food security, and poverty reduction instead of misplaced p
Read More: https://punchng.com/pdp-faults-diris-n100bn-stadium-project-demands-jobs-food-security/

item.title: The Open: Full R2 groupings and tee times for Royal Birkdale
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Groupings and tee times for the second round of The 154th Open, held at Royal Birkdale in Southport, Merseyside.

item.title: Court convicts three for obstructing FRSC operatives in Kano
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Three individuals have been convicted in Kano for obstructing FRSC operatives and inciting the public. Learn about the court’s judgment and the penalties f
Read More: https://punchng.com/court-convicts-three-for-obstructing-frsc-operatives-in-kano/

item.title: Suspend Umahi pending probe into Mary Habila’s death, Atiku urges Tinubu

item.description:
Atiku Abubakar calls on President Tinubu to suspend Works Minister David Umahi pending investigation into the death of 26-year-old Mary Habila. Nigerians d
Read More: https://punchng.com/suspend-umahi-pending-probe-into-mary-habilas-death-atiku-urges-tinubu/

item.title: One injured as suspected herders raid Benue community
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Suspected herders attacked Ankpechi community in Benue, injuring one person and looting shops. Learn more about the latest Benue herders attack.
Read More: https://punchng.com/one-injured-as-suspected-herders-raid-benue-community/

item.title: Why Omnichannel Communication is Becoming a Critical Defence against Digital Fraud
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: | By: Olatayo Ladipo-Ajai, country manager at Infobip Nigeria Digital payments may be accelerating the growth of West Africa’s economy, but with new fraud tactics emerging, organisations are increasingly relying on omnichannel communication to protect both customers and brands. Criminals are re

item.title: Dangote Refinery breaks silence on alleged petrol gantry sales suspension
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The clarification came on Thursday after reports emerged that the 700,000-barrel-per-day refinery had stopped loading petrol through its gantry, with the The post Dangote Refinery breaks silence on alleged petrol gantry sales suspension appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: ICPC Chairman Calls For Tech-Driven Anti-Corruption Strategy
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Chairman of the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC), Dr. Musa Adamu Aliyu has called for innovative, technology-driven approaches to combating corruption, warning that Nigeria cannot tackle modern corruption with outdated methods. Speaking at the IFEPS/ICPC Sta

item.title: Alcaraz set to make return from wrist injury at Cincinnati Open in August
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz has entered next month's ‌Cincinnati Open, which is live on Sky Sports, as he targets a return from a long-term wrist ‌injury.

item.title: Osun poll: ‘Allow us to vote first,’ PWDs urge INEC
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Persons with Disabilities (PWDs) in Osun State have appealed to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to give them priority by allowing them to vote first during the August 15 governorship election. The post Osun poll: ‘Allow us to vote first,’ PWDs urge INEC appeared first on The Sun

item.title: Peter Obi’s ADC exit left Southeast disadvantaged – Kenneth Okonkwo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A chieftain of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Kenneth Okonkwo, has said the departure of former Labour Party presidential candidate Peter Obi from the party's presidential race left the Southeast politically disadvantaged and complicated the process that produced former Rivers State Governor

The National Industrial Court dismisses FG’s application to stay the reinstatement of Prof. Monday Igwe as Enugu hospital chief, citing an incompetent appe

item.title: Children among dead at Algeria foster care home blaze - president
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Eleven people have died and 19 other have suffered injuries in the overnight fire, the authorities say.

item.title: Adamawa Court sentences father to 30 years’ imprisonment for impregnating daughter
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Upper Area Court I, sitting in Ganye, Adamawa State, has convicted and sentenced a man, Jediel Sylvester, to 30 years’ imprisonment for having sexual relations with his daughter and getting her pregnant. The court, presided over by Hon. Kabiru Musa, sentenced the defendant to 15 years&#821

item.title: Contracts: Senate seeks establishment of NASS tenders board
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Senate has directed the immediate establishment of a Tenders Board for the National Assembly as part of measures to ensure transparency... The post Contracts: Senate seeks establishment of NASS tenders board appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Shape 2027 contest through active participation, Reps charge youths
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The House of Representatives has urged Nigerian youths to shape the 2027 general election by participating actively in the electoral process. The post Shape 2027 contest through active participation, Reps charge youths appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Is SpaceX’s stock a bust because it fell below $135? Look what happened after Meta’s IPO.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Close to half of major IPOs sink below their offering price — and stay there for several years. Here’s why SpaceX is no different.

item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Oluwatosin Demehin has declared that the Super Falcons are fully motivated to defend their crown at the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations, Completesports.com reports. Nigeria will be aiming to secure a record-extending 11th continental title when the tournament gets underway in Morocco next w

item.title: ‎“I Talk To My Wife Every Day, Can’t Live Without Her” – Davido Opens Up On His Love For Chioma
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎Music star Davido has professed his love for his wife, Chioma, saying that he cannot live without her. ‎While on a livestream with popular streamer, Davrel, he spoke about the close bond he shares with his wife, revealing that they talk every day. According to him, no matter where he is in the worl

item.title: Italian officials handed jail terms for Genoa bridge disaster that killed 43
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The ex-head of Italy's motorway operator Giovanni Castellucci was handed a 12-year term over the 2018 bridge collapse.

item.title: Flood ravages Calabar communities, destroys property worth over N50m
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ike Uchechukwu, CALABAR Residents of Arab and Atimbo communities in Calabar Municipality Local Government Area of Cross River State are counting their losses after torrential rainfall triggered flooding that destroyed property estimated at over N50 million. The flood, which followed hours of heav

item.dc:creator: Vanguard

item.title: Troops foil kidnap attempt in Benue, recover three motorcycles
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Sector 1 of Operation WHIRL STROKE, OPWS, have thwarted a kidnap attempt after responding swiftly to a distress call from communities in Ukum Local Government Area of Benue State. According to security sources, the incident occurred at about 11:30 a.m. on July 14 when soldiers stationed at

item.title: CBN Introduces digital tracker to monitor BDC forex transactions
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has launched a new system to monitor how Bureau De Change (BDC) operators buy foreign exchange in the country.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Why Fans Want More Data Behind Every Digital Result
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Every goal now arrives with a stream of supporting information: the match clock, scorer, assist, substitutions, bookings, formations and updated tournament paths. The post World Cup 2026: Why Fans Want More Data Behind Every Digital Result appeared first on Channels Television.

item.description: Travel agency Fora announced a $60 million Series D round led by Forerunner and Tactile Ventures, valuing the company at $1 billion.

item.title: Stop seeing your duty as mere peacekeeping, Mutfwang urges military
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Plateau State Governor Caleb Mutfwang urges the Nigerian military to view the wave of Plateau killings as a threat to sovereignty, not mere peacekeeping.

item.description: The apex bank disclosed this in its regulatory guidance on purchase of FX by BDCs through authorized dealer banks in NFEM. The post CBN directs BDCs to sell unutilized FX to NFEM appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Minister of Works, David Umahi, has publicly reacted to the death of physiotherapist Mary Habila, who was found dead at a staff chalet within the premises of his residence in Uburu, Ebonyi State, saying there was no attempt to cover up the incident and that an autopsy had been ordered to determine t

item.title: Sam Neill’s Cause Of Death Confirmed As Family Announces Private Memorial
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Veteran actor Sam Neill, widely celebrated for his role as Dr. Alan Grant in the ‘Jurassic Park’ films, has died at the age of 78 after battling pneumonia. His representative confirmed the cause of death in a statement, revealing that Neill had previously overcome angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma,

item.description: The impressive performance and growing international acceptance of the Naira Card have fundamentally transformed global perceptions of Nigeria... item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Jeff Agbodo, ABAKALIKI The Ebonyi State Ministry of Capital City Development and Urban Planning has demolished unapproved structures erected along major roads and streets in Abakaliki metropolis. The exercise was part of the ministry’s ongoing building control and environmental sanitation enforce

item.title: Mortgage rates jump to the highest level of 2026
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Mortgage rates edged up to the highest level in a year, delivering a fresh blow to the housing market.

item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, in Niger State has assured residents that it is addressing the shortcomings that affected previous elections, expressing confidence that the 2027 general elections will be more credible, transparent, inclusive and secure, with no eligible voter di

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Suspected armed herders on Thursday stormed Ankpechi community in Oglewu Ward, along the Otukpo-Oweto-Abuja Road in Ohimini Local Government Area of Benue State, shot at residents and looted several provision stores. The attackers were said to have stormed the area and fired sporadically forcing res

Be Hot.” campaign, Infinix has officially announced the nationwide availability of the HOT 70 Pro, its newest smartphone designed for a generation that values creativity, individuality and intelligent innovation. item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: Senate Passes Bill Proposing ₦100,000 Fine for Major Traffic Violations The Senate has passed the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) (Amendment) Bill, 2026, proposing significantly stiffer penalties for a range of traffic offences, including fines of up to ₦100,000 for major violations. The bill is ai

item.title: Mary Habila’s death: Atiku fires message to Tinubu, says Umahi must step aside
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: It took the courage of Sahara Reporters to bring this death into public view. Three weeks after her death, no autopsy has been performed.

item.title: Citi Traders Pitch Shorting French Shipping Giant CMA CGM Bonds
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Citigroup’s credit traders and analysts are recommending their hedge fund clients short bonds issued by French shipping giant CMA CGM Group, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Nigeria’s Foreign Reserves Surge to Record $51.89 Billion
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Nigeria’s gross foreign reserves have reached a significant multi-year milestone, climbing to $51.89 billion as of Wednesday, July 15, 2026, according to the latest data from the Central Bank of Nigeria. This represents a steady upward trajectory throughout the first half of 2026, reflecting a robus

item.title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: With this new update, Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions and into completing tasks across the apps they use regularly.

item.title: Google continues its renaming streak by turning NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Google said users can soon access their notebooks through AI Mode in Search.

item.title: Google is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Google is giving its AI note-taking app a new name. The company announced on Thursday that NotebookLM is becoming Gemini Notebook, but will remain a standalone app even as it integrates more deeply across Gemini and Google Search. Google first revealed Gemini Notebook - then called Project Tailwind

item.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

item.title: Ghana, Senegal and Morocco: Why Ukraine’s strikes on Russian exports could drive up fuel costs
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: By targeting Russia’s refineries alongside its ports and grain export routes, Ukraine’s expanding drone campaign is disrupting diesel supplies and wheat shipments, exposing African economies to higher costs and renewed questions about their dependence on Moscow.

item.title: NANS at loggerheads with Wukari varsity management over ‘anti-student’ policies
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) has chided the management of the Federal University Wukari in Taraba State over a directive barring students with unpaid school fees from writing examinations, describing the policy as "anti-student" and "cruelty in its purest form." The post NANS

item.title: Prince William And Prince Harry Still Not Speaking Despite Harry’s Reunion With King Charles
item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: Iran reportedly directs Houthis to prepare Red Sea closure if US targets power infrastructure
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Iran has reportedly instructed Yemen’s Houthi movement to be prepared to shut down the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a critical gateway to the Red Sea, if the United States launches strikes on Iran’s power infrastructure, according to three sources familiar with the matter. The directive was dis

item.title: UK Minister Peter Kyle Demands FIFA Probe Over Argentina’s Falklands Banner
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The UK government has called on FIFA to investigate Argentina’s national team after its players displayed a banner reading “Las Malvinas son Argentinas” (“The Falklands are Argentine”) following their 2-1 victory over England in the FIFA World Cup semi-final. According

item.title: Paschi Casts Doubts Over Intesa Bid, Suggests Studying BPM Plan
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA’s board of directors raised broad concerns over a takeover bid by Intesa Sanpaolo SpA, saying the price offered appears low and that it relies on optimistic assumptions while exposing shareholders to risks.

item.description: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) and the European Union (EU) have urged the federal government to sustain ongoing economic reforms, strengthen institutions and improve data transparency if Nigeria is to attain an investment-grade sovereign credit rating and significantly reduce its co

item.title: Former Safaricom executive Michael Mutiga named Stanbic Bank Kenya CEO
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Mutiga joins Stanbic after serving as Chief Business Development and Strategy Officer at Safaricom, Kenya's largest telecommunications company and operator of the M-PESA mobile money platform.

item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Lagos Rail Mass Transit (LRMT) Blue Line has transported more than 8 million passengers in over 1,000 days of operation, while Phase II of the project has reached a major construction milestone with the successful installation of its first precast T-beam. item.title: Chevron to Sign Oil Investment Accords With Iraq on Friday
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Chevron Corp. expects to sign accords with Iraq on Friday that will advance its negotiations about investing in large oil fields, according to a senior company executive.

item.title: What next for England? How does Tuchel fix key problems for Euro 2028?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: The manner of England's World Cup exit was meek, and Thomas Tuchel must now find a way to fix those problems as focus will soon turn to qualifying for Euro 2028.

item.title: Spain Extends Welcome Mat for Chinese Carmakers to Its Workers
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Spain’s biggest Chinese industrial investment will rely on workers brought in from China during construction, according to a government report that lays out Madrid’s blueprint to bolster its auto industry.

item.title: Super Lig: Osimhen set to become Galatasaray captain
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Victor Osimhen is on course to become the new captain of Turkish champions Galatasaray following the departure of Mauro Icardi, marking another significant milestone in the Nigerian striker's growing influence at the club.

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Thousands of Ukrainians have taken to the streets in several cities to protest the removal of Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, a move that has sparked public anger and concerns over instability within the country’s military leadership. In Kyiv on Thursday, more than 1,000 demonstrators gathered at

item.title: Africa Declares a New Standard for its Communications Profession
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: On World PR Day, Africa’s professional communication bodies announce The Africa Declaration on the Professionalisation of Public Relations and Responsible Communication – a founding charter for continental self-governance, ethical practice, and shared professional standards. In a profess

item.title: Senate Rescinds Passage of Proceeds of Crime Amendment Bill for Fresh Scrutiny
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Sunday Aborisade in Abuja  The Senate on Thursday rescinded its earlier decision passing the Proceeds of Crime Act (Amendment) Bill, 2026, citing the need to address substantive drafting, legal and

item.title: Cleric Says 2021 Prophecy Foretold South Africa Xenophobic Crisis
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The founder of Abuja-based Everlasting Light of Hope International Ministry (The ELOHIM), Prophet Wiseman Daniel, has said prophetic warnings he issued in 2021 about rising xenophobic tensions in South Africa have been fulfilled following recent attacks on foreign nationals in the country. The cleri

item.title: EU Orders Google to Share Search Data, Open Android to Rival AI Services
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The European Union has ordered Google to share search data with competing search engines and make its Android operating system more accessible to rival artificial intelligence services, in the bloc’s latest move to rein in Big Tech dominance. The directive, issued on Thursday, was made under the EU’

item.title: Hamas leadership run-off expected between Meshaal and al-Hayya
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Israeli assassinations of several key leaders force Hamas to find new head, a process expected to be completed next week

item.title: Meta Deploys AI to Detect Teen Suicide Chats, Alert Parents
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Meta has introduced a new safety feature that alerts parents when its artificial intelligence chatbot detects that a teenager may be discussing suicide or self-harm, as technology companies face increasing pressure to strengthen safeguards around young users’ interactions with generative AI. T

item.title: 2027: Kogi High Court denies ordering deregistration of NDC
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The High Court of Justice in Kogi State has made new clarifications and denied reports claiming that it ordered the deregistration of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC.A statement by the Court’s Chief Information Officer, Saqeeb Saeed, on Thursday, described the allegation as false and misleading

item.title: Time’s running out to save $10 on Splatoon Raiders physical preorders
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Splatoon Raiders’ July 23rd launch day is almost here, so your window to preorder the physical version at a $10 discount is about to close. The digital version is $49.99, and so is the game on cartridge if you preorder at Walmart. Amazon previously honored the preorder discount, but it’s selling the

item.title: Court Orders Final Forfeiture Of N8.9bn Assets Linked To Achimugu
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) High Court sitting in Apo, Abuja, has ordered the final forfeiture of assets worth over N8.9bn linked to businesswoman, Aisha Achimugu, to the Federal Government. Justice Jude Onwugbuzie granted the order on Thursday following an application by the Economic and Fi

item.title: Chip Stocks Pummeled. SK Hynix Dives. SOX Socked Again.
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: 10 African countries with the strongest military in 2026
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria has been ranked as the third-strongest military power in Africa in the 2026 Global Firepower Index, behind Egypt and Algeria. The post 10 African countries with the strongest military in 2026 appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Michael Burry's $39 a month Substack hits 300,000 subscribers, fueling speculation of a $113M-a-year newsletter empire
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance
